# SpecStage

> AI-assisted, engineer-controlled tailoring of UFGS (Unified Facilities Guide Specifications) for federal construction projects. Currently in private beta.

SpecStage is a Windows desktop application that streamlines the tailoring of UFGS specifications for MILCON and other federal construction work. Engineers upload their project documents into a single workspace; SpecStage recommends the relevant UFGS specification sections, tailors them against the project context, and presents the result for engineer review. Approved output exports as SpecsIntact-compatible files or PDF.

The product is in private beta as of 2026. Beta access requests: hello@specstage.com.

## Pages

- [Home](https://www.specstage.com/): Product overview, capability summary, request access.
- [How it works](https://www.specstage.com/how-it-works): The end-to-end engineer workflow from document upload through review and export.
- [About](https://www.specstage.com/about): Mission, principles, "by engineers, for engineers" framing.
- [Request access](https://www.specstage.com/request-access): Apply for the private beta. Four short questions about UFGS work, tailoring frequency, SpecsIntact use, and a section to test.

## Key facts

- **Audience**: Federal construction engineers, A/E firms working on USACE, NAVFAC, AFCEC, and other DoD projects.
- **Compatibility**: Exports as SpecsIntact-compatible files for direct integration into existing SpecsIntact workflows, or as PDF for distribution and archival.
- **Approach**: AI-assisted, engineer-controlled. Recommendations are presented for review at every stage; engineers retain final approval authority on all output.
- **Traceability**: Every tailoring decision is recorded with its rationale and the project context it was based on.
- **Trademark notice**: SpecsIntact is a registered trademark of NASA. SpecStage is not affiliated with NASA.

## Contact

- General: hello@specstage.com
- Beta access: hello@specstage.com (mention role, organization, typical projects)
